headline: GE Vernova vs. AES: Which Stock Leads the Clean Energy Race?
summary: Both AES and GE Vernova ride on surging clean power demand from AI data centers, with renewables, grid upgrades and PPAs shaping their growth outlook.

raw.headline: GE Vernova vs. AES: Which Stock Leads the Clean Energy Race?

headline: DPL LLC Announces Extension of Expiration Time for Previously Announced Consent Solicitation
summary: DPL LLC (f/k/a DPL Inc.) ("DPL") today announced that it has extended the expiration time for its previously announced solicitation of consents (the "Consent Solicitation") from registered holders (the "Holders") of its 4.35% Senior Notes due 2029 (the "Notes") to 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on March 27, 2026, unless earlier terminated (such time and date, as it may be extended, the "Expiration Time").

raw.headline: DPL LLC Announces Extension of Expiration Time for Previously Announced Consent Solicitation

headline: IPALCO Enterprises, Inc. Announces Extension of Expiration Time for Previously Announced Consent Solicitations
summary: IPALCO Enterprises, Inc. ("IPALCO") today announced that it has extended the expiration time for each of its previously announced solicitations of consents (each, a "Consent Solicitation" and, collectively, the "Consent Solicitations") from registered holders (the "Holders") of its 4.25% Senior Notes due 2030 (the "2030 Notes") and 5.75% Senior Notes due 2034 (the "2034 Notes" and, together with the 2030 Notes, the "Notes") to 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on March 27, 2026, unless earlier term

headline: AES Announces Extension of Consent Solicitation for its 2028 Notes and Expiration of Consent Solicitations for its 2030 Notes and 2031 Notes
summary: The AES Corporation (the "Company" or "AES") (NYSE: AES) today announced that it has extended the expiration time for its previously announced solicitation of consents (the "Consent Solicitation") from registered holders of its 5.450% Senior Notes due 2028 (the "2028 Notes") to 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on March 27, 2026, unless earlier terminated (such time and date, as it may be extended, the "Expiration Time").

headline: Maximo Completes 100 MW of Robotic Solar Installation
summary: Maximo, the solar robotics company incubated by The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES), today announced the successful installation of 100 megawatts (MW) of utility-scale solar capacity at AES' Bellefield complex.
